  "findings": [
    "Lockout/Tagout isolation is incomplete: lock applied without a tag, or tag applied without a lock, violating the dual-protection sequence."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop all work immediately.",
    "Apply both a standardized lock and a completed tag to each energy isolating device.",
    "Verify zero energy state before resuming any service or maintenance work."
  ],
  "rationale": "Under OSHA 1910.147, lockout and tagout devices must be used together to ensure full protection unless a lockout-only program is specifically authorized and meets all criteria. Having a lock without a tag (or vice versa) during an active in-progress maintenance event fails to communicate critical safety information and violates standard safety sequences, presenting a critical risk of accidental re-energization.",
  "references": [
    "OSHA 1910.147(c)(4)",
    "OSHA 1910.147(c)(5)",
    "OSHA 1910.147(d)"
  ]
}
